title: WiseTech Founder Denies Human Trafficking
summary: Good morning from a very chilly Melbourne. SpaceX’s stock has come back to earth with a thud, tumbling 23% in just three days and wiping more than $600 billion off its market value. The selloff comes as the company prepares its first-ever investment-grade bond sale to help fund its growing AI ambitions.- Keira Wright, Australian Energy Reporter

title: Iran Oil Waiver Offers Lifeline to Tehran as Talks Proceed
summary: The US issued a 60-day license allowing Iran to sell oil on the international market, giving Tehran an economic lifeline as the two adversaries continued talks for a permanent peace deal.

title: US-Japan More Aligned on FX, Katayama Says After Bessent Talks
summary: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said she had talks over the phone with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, a move that only gave the yen a short-lived boost as it hovers near the weakest level in four decades.

title: Yen Intervention Risk Rises After Katayama-Bessent Talks
summary: Currency traders are on high alert for intervention after further weakness in the yen and a call between Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

title: Asia Stocks Follow Wall Street Lower as Tech Drops: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks tracked technology-led losses on Wall Street with investors continuing to monitor developments in US-Iran peace talks.

title: ‘A Lot of Leeway’: SpaceX’s High-Grade Debt Brings Out Skeptics
summary: When Moody’s Ratings first evaluated Nvidia Corp. almost a decade ago, it settled on a Baa1 investment-grade rating, taking comfort in its relatively light debt load and more than $1 billion of free cash flow after 16 years as a public company.

title: IFM Investors Nears 50% Stake in Atlas Arteria as Deadline Looms
summary: IFM Investors is nearing a 50% holding in Australia-listed toll road operator Atlas Arteria Ltd., ahead of the Thursday night deadline on its takeover bid.

title: ‘FOMO Really Got Me’: Taiwanese Go Deep Into Debt to Amp 100% Stock Rally
summary: Taiwan's locals are borrowing so much to invest in the TSMC-fueled AI stock frenzy it's raising fears of a bubble.

title: Oil Steadies as Iran Sales Waiver Signals Peace Talks Progress
summary: Oil steadied after falling more than 3% in the previous session following early progress in peace talks over the Iran war, which included a US waiver allowing some sales from the Islamic Republic.

title: Gold Steadies as US and Iran Flag Early Progress in Peace Talks
summary: Gold steadied after the US and Iran flagged early progress in negotiations to end the war that’s upended global markets and stoked inflation.

title: CFTC Seeks Input on 24/7 Futures, Energy Perpetuals Trades
summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission asked for public input on both perpetual contracts and 24/7 trading for traditional energy derivatives, after industry heavyweights raised concerns about offshore platforms and excessive levels of risk.

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/20/2026
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Associated Press International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Eurasia Group and GZERO Media President & Founder Ian Bremmer, International Crisis Group, Iran Project Director & Senior Advisor to the President Ali Vaez, Axios Defense Reporter Colin Demarest, Lawrence, Kansas Mayor Brad Findeldei, Oliver Wyman Partner & Global Anti-Financial Crime Practice Leader Daniel Tannebaum, Georgia Senator Raphael Warnock and “History That Doesn’t Suck!” Podcast Host, “Been There, Done That” Author and Utah Valley University Center for Constitutional Studies America 250 Professor Greg Jackson. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Links Offshore, Onshore Yuan in Tweak Toward Broader Use
summary: China is taking another step in its longstanding push to build a more global currency, bringing its domestic and offshore yuan markets a little closer together.

title: Japan’s Retail FX Traders Bet Their Government Can Prop Up Yen
summary: Japan’s army of retail currency traders have heeded their government’s warning and stopped betting on the yen to decline, putting them at odds with professional investors positioning for further weakness.

title: Previ Plans Hands-Off Approach to Future Vale Chairperson Picks
summary: Brazil’s largest pension fund will no longer seek to control selection of Vale SA’s chairperson and wants the iron ore miner to be overseen by an independent board leader.

title: Los Angeles Warehouse Fire Smoke Chokes Southern California for Fifth Day
summary: A fire at a cold storage warehouse near downtown Los Angeles continued pumping fumes across Southern California five days after it ignited, prompting emergency declarations as air quality reached unhealthy levels.

title: USA Rare Earth Rebuts MP Materials Suit as Rivalry Deepens
summary: USA Rare Earth Inc. pushed back against allegations by MP Materials Corp., calling a lawsuit filed by its rival an attempt to hinder the company’s growth.

title: UK Winter Power Supply Secure Despite Iran Disruption, Says Neso
summary: The UK will have secure power supplies this winter despite the disruption to energy markets from the Iran war, according to the National Energy System Operator.

title: Gas Stations Accused of Using AI to Boost California Prices
summary: A group of California consumers claimed in a lawsuit that gas station owners including Walmart Inc., Marathon Petroleum Corp., BP Plc and 7-Eleven Inc. are using artificial intelligence to illegally manipulate pump prices in the state that already has the highest rates in the US.

title: Avis Set for $650 Million Pentwater Payout After Short Squeeze
summary: Avis Budget Group Inc. is set to receive $650 million in cash as part of a settlement agreement with Pentwater Capital Management to resolve a lawsuit regarding short-swing profits, according to a filing.

title: US Refiners Get Rare Chance to Buy Iranian Crude Again
summary: The Trump administration has temporarily eased some sanctions on Iranian oil, pausing hostilities that have defined US-Iran relations since the 1979 hostage crisis.

title: Mexico Raises $6.3 Billion in New Bonds to Fund Buyback
summary: Mexico raised $6.3 billion in bonds to fund a buyback of its existing debt as credit-rating firms warn over the nation’s widening fiscal deficit, raising concerns over its investment-grade status.

title: Schick Razor Maker Is Said to Reject Unsolicited Takeover Offer
summary: Edgewell Personal Care Co., which makes Schick razors, has rejected an unsolicited takeover offer from private equity firm Yellow Wood Partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Cotopaxi CEO: Luggage a ‘Natural Extension’ for Brand
summary: Lindsay Shumlas, CEO of Cotopaxi, discusses the company's recent expansion from backpacks into the luggage market with the launch of their new Coraza luggage line. The product, introduced about two months ago, emphasizes sustainability and durability through a repair-led model, contrasting with the typical replacement-focused luggage industry. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: CME Direct Trading Platform Is Back Up After Earlier Disruptions
summary: Trading on CME Group Inc.’s CME Direct platform is back up after a brief disruption Monday as it experienced technical difficulties, according to traders.

title: Espriella’s Narrow Win Caps Post Election Gains in Colombia
summary: The rally sparked by Abelardo de la Espriella’s win in Colombian elections faded just hours into the session as investors worried that one of the tightest presidential elections in the nation’s history will limit his mandate to implement market-friendly policies.

title: Qualcomm Nears Deal for AI Chip Startup Modular
summary: Qualcomm Inc. is in advanced talks to acquire Modular Inc. in a transaction valuing the artificial intelligence chip startup at about $4 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Hedge Funds Piled Into Bearish Oil Bets Ahead of US-Iran MOU
summary: Hedge funds boosted bearish bets on US crude to the most in nearly five months on expectations that a preliminary peace deal between Washington and Tehran will boost Middle East oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
summary: 円が乱高下、イラン原油販売容認、急転直下の退場、マエストロ逝く、AI投資加速へ
